questions.q: Which sentence is a general conditional?
opts: If you heat water, it boils.
If you heated water, it would boil yesterday.
If you had heated water, it boiled.
If you will heat water, it boils.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "If you heat water, it boils"

questions.q: Which sentence is a first conditional?
opts: If it rains, we stayed home.
If it rains, we will stay home.
If it rained, we will stay home.
If it had rained, we would stay home.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "If it rains, we will stay home"

questions.q: Which sentence is a second conditional?
opts: If I am rich, I will travel.
If I were rich, I would travel.
If I was rich, I will travel.
If I had been rich, I would travel.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "If I were rich, I would travel"

questions.q: Choose the correct sentence with wish:
opts: I wish I have more time.
I wish I had more time.
I wish I will have more time.
I wish I having more time.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "I wish I had more time"

questions.q: Which modal expresses strong obligation?
questions.exp: Correct answer: must

questions.q: Which sentence shows agreement?
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "I completely agree with you"

questions.q: Which sentence shows disagreement?
opts: I see your point, but I disagree.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "I see your point, but I disagree"

questions.q: Which adjective has a negative meaning?
questions.exp: Correct answer: careless

questions.q: Choose the correct sentence with reported speech:
opts: He said that he was tired.
He said, “I was tired” yesterday now.
He told that he was tired.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "He said that he was tired"

questions.q: Which structure is correct?
questions.exp: Correct answer: It is important to study

questions.q: Which sentence uses an adverb of manner correctly?
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "She sings beautifully"

questions.q: Which sentence is correct with used to?
opts: I used to play football when I was younger.
I use to played football when I was younger.
I used to playing football when I was younger.
I am used play football when I was younger.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "I used to play football when I was younger"

questions.q: Which phrasal verb is related to travel?
questions.exp: Correct answer: take off

questions.q: Which expression is best for beginning an informal letter?
questions.exp: Correct answer: Dear Ahmed,

questions.q: Which expression is best for ending an informal letter?
questions.exp: Correct answer: Best wishes,

questions.q: Which sentence is in the passive voice?
opts: The lesson was explained by the teacher.
The teacher was explain the lesson.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "The lesson was explained by the teacher"

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses a relative pronoun?
opts: The man which lives next door is a pilot.
The man who lives next door is a pilot.
The man whose lives next door is a pilot.
The man where lives next door is a pilot.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "The man who lives next door is a pilot"

questions.q: Which sentence is a third conditional?
opts: If I study, I will pass.
If I studied, I would pass.
If I had studied, I would have passed.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "If I had studied, I would have passed"

questions.q: Which phrasal verb with take is correct?
questions.exp: Correct answer: take after

questions.q: A body idiom is an expression that includes:
questions.exp: Correct answer: a part of the body

questions.q: Which sentence best sets the scene for a narrative?
opts: It was a dark, windy night, and the streets were empty.
In conclusion, I strongly believe this is true.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "It was a dark, windy night, and the streets were empty"

questions.q: Which linking word is best for sequencing information?
questions.exp: Correct answer: then

questions.q: Which sentence is best for asking an eyewitness for information?
opts: I do not care what happened.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "What exactly did you see?"

questions.q: Which sentence is best for taking a message?
opts: Hold on, I’ll write that down.
I can’t hear you because I don’t want to.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "Hold on, I’ll write that down"

questions.q: Which sentence is best for leaving a message?
opts: Call me back when you can, please.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "Call me back when you can, please"

questions.q: Which clause shows cause?
questions.exp: Correct answer: because he was tired

questions.q: Which clause shows opposition?
questions.exp: Correct answer: although she was tired

questions.q: Which sentence uses inversion correctly?
opts: Never I have seen such a place.
Never have I seen such a place.
Never I seen have such a place.
Have never I seen such a place.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "Never have I seen such a place"

questions.q: Which sentence is most suitable for persuasive writing?
opts: In my opinion, schools should create more green spaces because they improve students’ wellbeing.
The boy was wearing a blue shirt.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "In my opinion, schools should create more green spaces because they improve students’ wellbeing"

questions.q: Which sentence is most suitable for expository writing?
opts: I think this is the best choice ever!
Solar energy is produced by converting sunlight into usable power.
You must agree with me now.
questions.exp: The correct sentence is: "Solar energy is produced by converting sunlight into usable power"
